description_en: Traditional Chinese four-poster canopy bed (Jiazi Chuang) with rectangular platform, four tall corner posts extending from floor to top rail, railings on three sides, and upper canopy frame. Constructed in dark hardwood with traditional mortise and tenon joinery.

components.name: bed_frame
components.position_note: At 0.45m height, supported by four corner posts

components.name: corner_post
components.description: Square vertical post extending from floor to canopy top, serving as both leg and canopy support

components.name: bed_board
components.description: Solid wooden sleeping surface or slatted base

components.name: headboard_panel
components.description: Decorative protective railing at head side
components.position_note: Between two head-side corner posts at 0.5m to 1.1m height

components.name: footboard_panel
components.description: Lower protective railing at foot side
components.position_note: Between two foot-side corner posts at 0.5m to 0.9m height

components.name: side_railing
components.position_note: Between front and back posts on left and right sides at 0.5m to 0.9m height

components.name: canopy_frame
components.position_note: At 2.0m height, resting on top of four corner posts

style_notes: Dark hardwood construction (hongmu or zitan). Corner posts feature slight inward taper (侧脚收分) and may terminate in simple carved finials above the canopy frame. Railing panels traditionally feature lattice openwork patterns (reticulated 冰裂纹 or geometric 回纹 designs) which should be represented as surface texture/decal rather than geometry. Joinery uses exposed mortise-tenon (榫卯) connections at post-frame intersections. Canopy frame has rounded edges and slightly overhangs posts. Mattress is traditional thin cotton pad, not modern thick spring mattress.
